title: lepidotis lateralis
intro: Lepidotis lateralis, also known as the Marsh Cress or Marsh Yellow Cress, is a perennial aquatic plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). Traditionally, Lepidotis lateralis has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as potentially supporting the treatment of conditions like arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Lepidotis lateralis is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, although it can also be used in tincture or salve form, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
